notes: Windows + Cursor CLI (agent) + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Implement feed CRUD API and file storage
userStories.description: Support creating, updating, deleting, and querying the feed list, and write to the data/feeds index.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests are written first and cover feed create/update/delete/query.
Deleting a feed deletes its corresponding directory.
API contract documentation is consistent with tests.

userStories.title: Implement RSS fetching and article persistence
userStories.description: Fetch articles per feed and provide queries in reverse chronological order, writing to data/feeds/{feedId}/articles.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover inserting new articles and idempotency for duplicates.
Article lists are sorted by published time in descending order.
Failures for one feed do not affect fetching for other feeds.

userStories.title: Implement summary profile management
userStories.description: Support configuring multiple AI summary profiles, including name/base_url/key/model/fields/prompt_template.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover creating, updating, deleting, and querying profiles.
The uniqueness constraint for profile names is clearly enforced.
The configuration storage structure is documented.

userStories.title: Implement manual triggering of article AI summaries
userStories.description: Allow users to select an article and a profile to trigger a summary, then write the result to a markdown file.
acceptanceCriteria: Templates support variables such as title and content.
Summary results are written to `.md` files.
Tests cover mocked AI calls and writing the output to disk.

userStories.title: Implement global cascading cleanup for profile delete/edit
userStories.description: When deleting or renaming a profile, remove all summary markdown and metadata with the same name under all feed articles.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover the full scope of global cleanup.
Summaries for other profiles are not accidentally deleted.
The cleanup process tolerates missing files.

userStories.title: Implement scheduled feed fetching
userStories.description: Run background tasks to fetch feeds at fixed intervals.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover the scheduling trigger logic.
Error isolation and logging are clear.
Manual triggers and scheduled tasks can coexist.

userStories.title: Implement basic frontend page flow
userStories.description: Build the page skeletons for feed management, article list, article summary, and summary configuration, and connect them to the APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: Routing and page structure are stable.
Key interactions are covered by tests.
Empty/loading/error states are visible.

userStories.title: Complete feed management interaction details
userStories.description: Refine feed management frontend behavior according to README user flow, including add/edit/delete/list refresh and user feedback states.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can add, edit, delete, and refresh feed list in one session, and UI state stays consistent after each operation.
Boundary/exception: Invalid URL, duplicate-like URL, and backend failure cases show explicit and actionable error feedback.
Regression: Existing feed list rendering and navigation entry points remain stable after interaction refinements.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for feed management flow
userStories.description: Create Windows-runnable UI automation coverage for feed management frontend flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: End-to-end case verifies add/edit/delete/refresh feed flow and resulting list state changes.
Boundary/exception automation: Invalid input and mocked API failure cases assert visible validation/error feedback.
Regression automation: Existing feed management route load and baseline list rendering are covered to prevent breakage.
Windows execution: Test command, prerequisites, and target suite scope are documented.

userStories.title: Complete article list browsing interaction details
userStories.description: Refine article list frontend behavior by feed, including loading, empty state, refresh and failure-recovery interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can enter feed article list, view loaded results, and refresh list without losing current context.
Boundary/exception: Empty list and fetch failure states are clearly visible, and retry behavior is available and stable.
Regression: Reverse chronological visibility and feed-to-list navigation behavior remain correct.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for article list flow
userStories.description: Create UI automation coverage for article list browsing flow and its key state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: Article list loading and refresh behavior is verified from feed entry to visible list rendering.
Boundary/exception automation: Empty-data and failed-fetch scenarios assert expected UI feedback and recovery actions.
Regression automation: Existing sort visibility and navigation behavior is covered with stable assertions.
Windows execution: Automation is runnable in local Windows workflow with documented command and scope.

userStories.title: Complete article summary page interaction details
userStories.description: Refine article summary frontend behavior for summary viewing and triggering operations with robust user feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can select summary profile, trigger generation, and view generated summary result successfully.
Boundary/exception: No-profile-selected, no-data, and generation-failure states provide explicit user-visible feedback.
Regression: Repeated trigger attempts and page revisit keep summary-related UI behavior stable and predictable.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for article summary flow
userStories.description: Create UI automation coverage for article summary viewing and triggering frontend flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: Profile selection and summary trigger flow validates in-progress and success UI feedback.
Boundary/exception automation: No-data and failed-generation scenarios assert expected message and interaction handling.
Regression automation: Existing summary view rendering and re-entry behavior are protected by automated checks.
Windows execution: Setup assumptions and run command are documented for local automation runs.

userStories.title: Complete summary profile management interaction details
userStories.description: Refine summary profile frontend behavior including create/edit/delete/list and validation/error feedback details.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can create, update, delete, and re-list summary profiles with consistent interaction feedback.
Boundary/exception: Required-field missing, name conflict, and backend failure cases are clearly handled in UI.
Regression: Existing profile list rendering and navigation behavior stays stable after interaction refinements.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for summary profile flow
userStories.description: Create UI automation coverage for summary profile management frontend flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: Profile create/update/delete/list flow is covered with deterministic assertions.
Boundary/exception automation: Validation prompts, conflict errors, and API failure feedback are asserted.
Regression automation: Existing profile management page load and baseline list behavior remain protected.
Windows execution: Test suite command and prerequisites are documented in frontend test instructions.

userStories.title: Establish Tailwind UI foundation
userStories.description: Set up Tailwind styling foundation for frontend, including design tokens, layout primitives, and reusable style conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Tailwind configuration, shared tokens, and base utility conventions are available and documented.
Boundary/exception: Legacy styles and Tailwind coexist without breaking current functional pages during transition.
Regression: Existing frontend behavior remains unchanged while foundational style layer is introduced.

userStories.title: Migrate core pages to Tailwind visual design
userStories.description: Apply Tailwind-based visual improvements to home, feed management, article list, article summary, and summary profile pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Core pages share consistent spacing, typography, and interaction styles after Tailwind migration.
Boundary/exception: Narrow-width layout and long-content scenarios remain readable and usable.
Regression: Page-level functionality and existing interaction paths stay stable after style migration.

userStories.title: Add UI verification for Tailwind visual regression
userStories.description: Add automated UI verification to guard against major visual regressions after Tailwind migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path verification: Critical pages and key UI states have automated visual/UI checks with stable baselines.
Boundary/exception verification: Responsive breakpoints and error/empty/loading states are included in verification scope.
Regression verification: Baseline update process is documented and prevents accidental acceptance of unexpected UI drift.
Windows execution: Verification command and integration point with frontend quality gate are documented.

userStories.title: Background process to add title_trans to articles via title_translation profile
userStories.description: Add a background process that gradually populates a title_trans field for articles. It uses a user-configured AI profile named "title_translation": the text to translate is passed as title; the returned result is split by " (ASCII double quote) and the last non-empty segment is taken as title_trans. title_trans is then shown instead of title in the article list and in the summary page header.
acceptanceCriteria: Background process runs and processes articles incrementally without blocking the main app.
Translation is performed via the configured "title_translation" profile; input is article title, output is parsed per spec (split by ", last non-empty = title_trans).
title_trans is persisted (e.g. in article data) and exposed to frontend.
Article list and summary page header display title_trans when present, otherwise fall back to title.

userStories.title: Article favorites with per-article flag and sorted list
userStories.description: Support favoriting articles: each article has a favorite icon at the start; clicking toggles favorite on/off. Implementation: add a marker file in the article folder to indicate favorite. Article list sort order: most recently favorited first, then earlier favorited, then by publish time (newest first, then older).
acceptanceCriteria: Each article row shows a favorite icon; click toggles favorite state (persisted via marker file in article folder).
Favorite state is reflected in list and survives refresh.
List order: recently favorited articles first, then earlier favorited, then by publish date descending.

userStories.title: Article list search filter (client-side)
userStories.description: Add a search control on the article list that filters articles by user input. Purely client-side filtering: no backend search; filter runs as the user types and updates the visible list in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: Search/filter input is visible on the article list page.
Typing filters the displayed articles in real time (e.g. by title or other visible fields).
Empty search shows all articles; no server round-trip for filtering.

userStories.title: Article list left-side date (year-month) with age-based fading
userStories.description: Show date (year-month) on the left of each article in the list. Visual treatment: the further in the past, the lighter the color; same-day darkest, articles older than three months lightest.
acceptanceCriteria: Each article row shows a date (year-month) on the left.
Color intensity reflects recency: same day darkest, gradually lighter for older dates, lightest for three months or older.
Date display is consistent with sort order and does not break layout.

userStories.title: Add virtual feed schema in feed storage
userStories.description: Extend feed storage schema to represent virtual feeds for article favorites collections with name + empty URL + explicit type marker.
acceptanceCriteria: Feed storage supports virtual feed records with name, empty URL, and a clear type/marker to distinguish from normal RSS feeds.
Existing feed read/write logic can load both normal and virtual feeds without schema break.
Schema/documentation updates describe virtual feed fields and compatibility behavior.
Existing normal RSS feed flow remains backward compatible and unaffected.

userStories.title: Implement virtual feed CRUD API endpoints
userStories.description: Add backend API support to create/list/delete virtual feeds while preserving existing feed API behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: API supports creating virtual feed with required name and empty URL persisted to disk.
API list/read responses include virtual feed records with distinguishable type marker.
Deleting virtual feed removes its corresponding feed directory subtree safely.
Existing normal RSS feed API behavior remains backward compatible.

userStories.title: Add "Article Favorites" button in feed management UI
userStories.description: On feed management UI, add an "Article Favorites" button beside RSS subscription entry and provide create-virtual-feed interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Feed management page shows an "Article Favorites" button adjacent to RSS subscription entry.
Clicking the button opens create-virtual-feed interaction requiring only feed name.
After successful creation, the new virtual feed appears in feed list with clear visual distinction from normal feeds.

userStories.title: Support virtual feed article list entry and baseline states
userStories.description: Allow opening virtual feed article list from feed list and show stable loading/empty/error states before custom article creation is enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can navigate from virtual feed item to its article list page.
Virtual feed article list handles loading, empty, and error states with visible feedback.
Navigation and baseline list rendering remain stable for both virtual and normal feeds.

userStories.title: Define custom article schema and persistence for virtual feeds
userStories.description: Define storage contract for custom articles under virtual feeds (including URL/title/content/time/source metadata) and persist consistently with existing article layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Custom article storage schema is defined with required and optional fields.
Custom article records are written under virtual feed article storage using existing article directory conventions.
Persisted custom articles can be loaded and rendered by article list APIs.

userStories.title: Add custom article create form in virtual feed article list
userStories.description: In virtual feed article list UI, add "Add custom article" entry and editable form fields for URL/title/content/publish time/source metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Virtual feed article list shows visible "Add custom article" interaction entry.
Create form supports editing URL/title/content/publish time/source metadata with partial empty fields allowed before submit.
Form submission invokes backend create API and keeps user-entered values unchanged before rule-based fill stage.

userStories.title: Implement URL-branch missing-field autofill in backend
userStories.description: When URL is provided for custom article creation, backend fetches/parses page content to fill only missing fields and never overwrite user-provided values.
acceptanceCriteria: If URL is provided and fields are missing, backend attempts URL fetch/parsing and fills empty fields only.
User-provided non-empty fields are never overwritten by autofill.
Autofill failure or incomplete required fields returns explicit actionable error response.

userStories.title: Finalize URL-branch create success and UI feedback
userStories.description: Complete frontend/backend integration for URL branch so creation succeeds in one submit when required fields become non-empty after autofill.
acceptanceCriteria: When URL autofill yields all required non-empty fields, article is created successfully in same submit flow.
UI shows clear success/failure feedback for URL-based creation attempts.
Created custom article appears in virtual feed article list after creation.

userStories.title: Implement no-URL mandatory validation rules
userStories.description: For custom article creation without URL, enforce title and content as mandatory fields and block creation when either is missing.
acceptanceCriteria: No-URL create path validates title and content as mandatory fields.
Missing title or content blocks creation and returns explicit validation messages.
Validation behavior is consistent across backend API and frontend form feedback.

userStories.title: Implement no-URL first-click default fill behavior
userStories.description: When URL is absent and title/content are present but other fields are empty, first click fills reasonable defaults and enters pending-confirm state without creating article.
acceptanceCriteria: No-URL path with title/content present and other empty fields triggers default fill on first click.
Reasonable defaults are applied (for example current timestamp and placeholder URL such as https://).
First click does not create article and UI clearly indicates pending confirmation state.

userStories.title: Implement no-URL second-click confirmation create flow
userStories.description: After default fill in no-URL path, allow users to review/edit values and create on second click when all required fields are non-empty.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can review and edit default-filled values before confirmation.
Second create click succeeds when required fields are non-empty.
UI feedback clearly differentiates first-click fill stage and second-click creation stage.

userStories.title: Add backend tests for virtual feed and custom article rules
userStories.description: Add backend automated tests covering virtual feed APIs and custom article create logic for URL branch and no-URL branch.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover virtual feed create/list/delete persistence behaviors.
Tests cover URL-branch autofill success and failure/incomplete-field scenarios.
Tests cover no-URL mandatory validation and two-step default-fill/confirm behavior.

userStories.title: Add frontend/UI automation for custom article create flows
userStories.description: Add frontend tests and UI automation for creating virtual feeds and custom articles, including URL autofill and no-URL two-step confirmation with regression checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend/UI automation covers create virtual feed and open virtual feed article list.
Automation covers URL branch custom article creation with visible success/failure feedback assertions.
Automation covers no-URL two-step flow (first-click fill, second-click confirm) and regression of existing RSS feed interactions.
Existing normal RSS feed flow remains backward compatible and unaffected.
